title: How a request moves through production.
description: A sticky walk through the stack I ship with — hover any node for purpose, usage, and the production story behind it.

nodes.label: Client
nodes.purpose: Collect intent and render the product surface people actually use.
nodes.usage: Browser and app clients initiating payments, content workflows, and AI actions.
nodes.challenges: Keeping UX responsive while backend work is async and eventually consistent.
nodes.story: Client interactions never assume a long request will finish in one round trip.

nodes.label: Angular
nodes.purpose: Structure complex product UI with predictable state and strong typing.
nodes.usage: Production admin and customer-facing surfaces for Credee and Practina.
nodes.challenges: Large feature surfaces that still feel fast under real data volume.
nodes.story: Feature modules stay isolated so payments and AI workstreams can evolve independently.

nodes.label: Node
nodes.purpose: Own business logic, auth boundaries, and orchestration across services.
nodes.usage: TypeScript APIs for FinTech flows, AI jobs, webhooks, and internal tools.
nodes.challenges: Protecting hot paths without turning every endpoint into a God function.
nodes.story: APIs validate, authorize, then decide — sync response or durable background work.

nodes.label: Redis
nodes.purpose: Cut latency and coordinate short-lived state without overloading MySQL.
nodes.usage: Session/cache layers, rate limits, and queue backing for high-churn keys.
nodes.challenges: Invalidation and backpressure when traffic spikes around payment events.
nodes.story: Hot reads leave the database alone until cache truth must be refreshed.

nodes.label: BullMQ
nodes.purpose: Make expensive or unreliable work durable, retryable, and observable.
nodes.usage: Payment side effects, AI generation, scheduling, and outbound integrations.
nodes.challenges: Idempotency, dead-letter handling, and visibility when jobs fail at 2am.
nodes.story: If it can fail, retry, or take time — it belongs on a queue, not in a request.

nodes.label: Workers
nodes.purpose: Execute domain jobs with isolation from the public request path.
nodes.usage: Background processors for Stripe events, OpenAI tasks, and media workflows.
nodes.challenges: Concurrency limits, poison messages, and keeping worker deploys safe.
nodes.story: Workers turn product intent into completed work without blocking the client.

nodes.label: Postgres/MySQL
nodes.purpose: Persist the source of truth for money, contracts, and product state.
nodes.usage: Transactional records for payments, entities, and audit-friendly writes — indexing and query tuning cut API P99 latency 60%.
nodes.challenges: Indexing, contention, and migrations under live production load.
nodes.story: Every critical mutation is written for recovery, not just for the happy path.

nodes.label: Stripe
nodes.purpose: Move money with a payment platform that can survive reality.
nodes.usage: Charges, fees, contracts-adjacent billing, and webhook-driven state changes.
nodes.challenges: Webhook ordering, retries, and fee edge cases that disagree with documents.
nodes.story: Payment state is driven by verified events — never by a single optimistic click.

nodes.label: Response
nodes.purpose: Return a clear outcome the client can trust and recover from.
nodes.usage: Accepted jobs, completed actions, and explicit pending states for async work.
nodes.challenges: Communicating in-flight work without pretending everything is already done.
nodes.story: A good response is honest about what finished now and what will finish next.
